acceptanceReviewed.criterion: A CLI or test helper can run as a sender using evidence/m2-nvenc-encode-sample/sample-av1.ivf.
A CLI or test helper can run as a receiver and validate frame metadata, payload byte count, and payload SHA-256.
A localhost integration test proves the sender/receiver exchange without requiring macOS hardware.
Evidence records commands, sample hash, sender log, receiver log, and a result/timeline artifact.
Docs explain the harness is not QUIC, not VideoToolbox decode, not Metal render, not presentation, and not latency proof.

realWorldValidation.evidence: This node requires localhost smoke proof only. It prepares later cross-device validation and makes no live Mac/LAN, QUIC, decode, render, presentation, or latency claim.
